err: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Erre.
few: determiner. An indefinite, but usually small, number of.
fie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fyer.
fir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Firan.
fire: noun. A (usually self-sustaining) chemical reaction involving the bonding of oxygen with carbon or other fuel, with the production of heat
firer: noun. A person who fires a weapon; a shooter.
firework: noun. A device using gunpowder and other chemicals which, when lit, emits a combination of coloured flames, sparks, whistles or bangs, and
for: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fore.
fore: adjective. Former; occurring earlier (in some order); previous.
fork: noun. Any of several types of pronged (tined) tools (physical tools), as follows: A utensil with spikes used to put solid food into the
forker: noun. An agricultural laborer who uses a fork.
forkier: adjective. comparative form of forky: more forky
fro: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Old French.
froe: noun. A cleaving tool for splitting cask staves and shingles from a block of wood.
frow: noun. A woman; a wife, especially a Dutch or German one.
ire: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iresim.
irk: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iraqw.
kef: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kpessi.
kefir: noun. A fermented milk drink from the Caucasus and Eastern Europe, similar to yogurt but more liquid.
kerf: noun. The act of cutting or carving something; a stroke or slice.
kif: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eastern Parbate Kham.
kir: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Kyrgyz.
koi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Komi-Permyak.
kor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Korean.
kore: noun. An Ancient Greek statue of a woman, portrayed standing, usually clothed, painted in bright colours and having an elaborate hairstyle.
oke: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Okpe (Southwestern Edo).
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
owe: verb. To be under an obligation to give something back to someone or to perform some action for someone.
rei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reli.
rework: noun. The act of redoing, correcting, or rebuilding.
rif: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tarifit.
rife: adjective. Widespread, common, prevalent, current (mainly of unpleasant or harmful things).
rifer: adjective. comparative form of rife: more rife
row: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dela-Oenale.
weir: noun. An adjustable dam placed across a river to regulate the flow of water downstream.
wife: noun. A married woman, especially in relation to her spouse.
wire: noun. Metal formed into a thin, even thread, now usually by being drawn through a hole in a steel die.
wirer: noun. A tool to assist in installing wire.
woe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Woleaian.
wok: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Longto.
woke: adjective. Awake: conscious and not asleep.
